microsoft_ft: Owned **end-to-end delivery** of Azure's Resilience Automation Platform (0→1), defining requirements and self-service scheduling workflows that cut operational toil by 30% and scaled a 2-person program to 45+ annual drills.

Defined activation and retention metrics for internal platform users, instrumenting execution flows and post-launch measurement to sustain **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, and SAP.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, scoping and shipping a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical defects and establishing a continuous validation model.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows to reduce cycle time by **39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, directly expanding the team's ability to ship and iterate without adding headcount.

Led cross-functional execution across 14 high-visibility recovery projects — managing scope, timelines, and dependencies — including a sovereign-cloud isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+** enterprise contract.
microsoft_2023: Drove **product adoption** for AI-powered code generation workflows by conducting user demos and training across 14 key teams, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and establishing durable return usage habits.

Conducted **11+ user research interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing roadmap prioritization to include intent-based YAML generation as a top-requested capability.

Improved data retrieval by migrating documentation to an AI-powered **semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and increasing session depth for engineering users.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across 140+ teams on a unified automation prioritization framework, **accelerating region launches by 28%** and generating $3M in incremental revenue.

Ran discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual toil** per region launch, then built a phased roadmap to automate critical paths — directly informing the product backlog and resource planning.

Engineered a **Power BI engagement dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps and prioritize the highest-impact improvements each quarter.
amazon_robotics: Defined the legacy OS migration strategy for a **2,000+ unit pilot**, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations and achieving zero operational downtime — a model later adopted for broader fleet rollout.
Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across Engineering and Operations.
Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating software deployment cycles by 25% and reducing release toil.
pro_painters: Increased job **bookings by 26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflow for 200+ monthly proposals, building a structured CRM process that improved activation and repeat engagement.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy — optimizing the website funnel and Google profile to surface the right offer to the right user.

Conducted **profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks, delivering pricing model recommendations that improved unit economics and informed leadership's annual planning.

tailoring_notes: Wove 'activation and retention metrics' directly into microsoft_ft bullet 2 — mirrors JD's 'activation, retention, and personalization' ownership language.
Used 'return usage habits' in microsoft_2023 bullet 1 and 'repeat engagement' in pro_painters bullet 1 — echoes JD's emphasis on 'return visit frequency' and 'establish return habits'.
Embedded 'user research interviews' (microsoft_2023 bullet 2) and 'discovery' (microsoft_2022 bullet 2) to mirror JD's 'qualitative research' and 'problem definition through scoping' framing.
Referenced 'semantic search' in microsoft_2023 bullet 3 and 'personalization/recommendation' in skills to align with JD's nice-to-have on personalization systems.
Used 'end-to-end delivery' in microsoft_ft bullet 1 and 'end-to-end scoping' in pro_painters bullet 1 to echo JD's repeated 'own projects end to end' requirement.
